name: Lentil Savoy
recipeIngredient: 1 head purple kale (salad savoy) - one bowl-full
recipeInstructions:
Lentil Savoy
Serves two to four people
1. Rinse the lentils and cook in 1 1/2 cups of vegetable stock.
Use a rice cooker for this step.
2. While cooking the lentils, cut the stems off the kale.
Chop stems into half-inch pieces.
3. Chop the red bell pepper.
4. Place the pepper and stems in a large pan with enough stock to cover
the bottom (about 1/4 inch). Cook on medium for a couple of minutes,
then turn the flame down.
5. Wash and chop mushrooms. Add them to the pan.
6. About 20-30 minutes after starting step 1 (around 5-10 minutes after
finishing step 5), add the cooked lentils to the pan. If using a rice
cooker, shut it off manually.
7. Add cinnamon and other spices as desired. Stir the mixture in the pan,
then add the kale leaves on top. Do not mix them in.
8. Cook for another 15 minutes or until the leaves have a buttery taste.
9. Remove the kale from the top and serve separately.
Serve with black pepper available at the table.
Try the kale leaves with some nutritional yeast.
Notes:
If no stock is available, add more spices in step 7.
Use wild mushrooms if possible for extra flavor (though they are more
expensive).
Purple kale is used as a garnish in salad bars.
